title: Self-describing proof envelope with named public signals

artifacts.description: Canonical specification for BolyraEnvelope wire format: version, circuit identifier, provingSystem enum, named signals schema per circuit, and codec contract
Core BolyraEnvelope type, encode()/decode() functions, fromRaw(circuit, rawSignals) migration helper, and per-circuit signal maps (HumanUniqueness, AgentPolicy, Delegation)

Exported const maps from circuit name to ordered signal name arrays, e.g. HUMAN_UNIQUENESS_SIGNALS = ['humanMerkleRoot', 'nullifierHash', 'nonceBinding']. Single source of truth for positional indexing.
Re-export BolyraEnvelope, encode, decode, fromRaw from public API surface
Python dataclass BolyraEnvelope with encode()/decode() (JSON-serializable dict), fromRaw() helper, and signal maps mirroring the TS definitions
Unit tests: round-trip encode/decode for all three circuits, fromRaw migration helper, version mismatch rejection, unknown circuit rejection, missing signal detection
Python pytest suite: round-trip, fromRaw, cross-compatibility with TS-encoded envelopes (JSON fixtures shared)
Shared JSON fixture files with canonical encoded envelopes for all three circuits, used by both TS and Python test suites to verify cross-SDK compatibility
Add BolyraEnvelope section showing migration from positional publicSignals[] to named signals, with fromRaw() one-liner upgrade path

steps: Define the BolyraEnvelope wire format in spec/proof-envelope-v1.md: version string (semver), circuit enum ('HumanUniqueness' | 'AgentPolicy' | 'Delegation'), provingSystem enum ('groth16' | 'plonk'), signals record keyed by name, opaque proof field

Create sdk/src/circuits/signal-maps.ts with ordered name arrays for each circuit derived from the authoritative public signal ordering in circuits/src/*.circom; add a lint check that signal count matches
Implement BolyraEnvelope TypeScript type and encode() in sdk/src/envelope.ts: accepts (circuit, provingSystem, rawProof, rawSignals[]) and returns versioned envelope with named signals

Implement decode() in sdk/src/envelope.ts: validates version, circuit, provingSystem fields; maps named signals back to bigint[]; throws typed errors (EnvelopeVersionError, UnknownCircuitError, SignalCountMismatch)
Implement fromRaw(circuit, provingSystem, rawProof, rawSignals[]) as a thin alias for encode() to serve as the migration helper for existing integrators using positional arrays
Wire BolyraEnvelope into existing proveHandshake() and verifyHandshake() return types so the SDK natively emits envelopes; keep a rawMode flag for backward compat

Mirror the spec in sdk-python/bolyra/envelope.py using dataclasses: BolyraEnvelope dataclass, encode() returns JSON-serializable dict, decode() accepts dict, fromRaw() helper; signal maps as module-level constants
Write shared JSON fixture files (sdk/test/fixtures/envelope-v1-samples.json) capturing canonical encoded envelopes for all three circuits with known signal values
Write TS unit tests in sdk/test/envelope.test.ts covering: round-trip for all circuits, fromRaw migration, version mismatch, unknown circuit, signal count mismatch, JSON serialization fidelity
Write Python pytest suite in sdk-python/tests/test_envelope.py loading the shared JSON fixtures to verify TS-encoded envelopes decode correctly in Python and vice versa
Update sdk/QUICKSTART.md with a migration guide: before/after code showing positional publicSignals[] replaced by named envelope fields, fromRaw() one-liner
Bump sdk package version (minor) and sdk-python version; update CHANGELOG entries for both packages
dependencies: sdk/src/index.ts (existing public API surface to extend)
